elements.sent-2411-007: The problem of approximating an underlying distribution from its observed samples is a fundamental scientific problem.
elements.sent-2411-008: The distribution learning problem has been studied for more than a century in statistics, and it is the underlying engine for much of applied machine learning.
elements.sent-2411-009: The emphasis in modern applications is on high-dimensional distributions, with the goal being to understand when one can escape the curse of dimensionality.
elements.sent-2411-010: The survey by \cite{diakonikolas2016learning gives an excellent overview of classical and modern techniques for distribution learning, especially when there is some underlying structure to be exploited.}
elements.sent-2411-011: In this work, we investigate how to go beyond worst case sample complexities for learning distributions.
elements.sent-2411-012: We consider the situation where the algorithm is also given the aid of possibly imperfect advice regarding the input distribution.
elements.sent-2411-013: We position our study in the context of algorithms with predictions, where the usual problem input is supplemented by ``predictions'' or ``advice'' (potentially drawn from modern machine learning models) and the algorithm's goal is to incorporate the advice in a way that improves performance if the advice is of high quality, but if the advice is inaccurate, there should not be degradation below the performance in the no-advice setting.

elements.algorithm-2411-001: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The ApproxL1 algorithm.} \label{alg:approxl1} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, block size $k \in [d]$, lower bound $\alpha > 0$, upper bound $\zeta > 2 \alpha$, and i.i.d.\ samples $\cS$ from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$, $\OK$, or $\lambda \in \R$ \State Define $w = \left\lceil {d}/{k} \right\rceil$ and $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$ \State Partition the index set $[d]$ into $w$ blocks: \[ \bB_1 = \{1, \ldots, k\}, \bB_2 = \{k+1, \ldots, 2k\}, \ldots, \bB_w = \{k(w-1)+1, \ldots, d\} \] \For{$j \in \{1, \ldots, w\}$} \State Define $\cS_{j} = \{ \bx_{\bB_j} \in \R^{|\bB_j|} : \bx \in \cS \}$ as the samples projected to $\bB_j$ \Comment{See \cref{def:projected-vector}} \State Initialize $o_j = \Fail$ \For{$i = 1, 2, \ldots, \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil$} \State Define $l_i = 2^{i-1} \cdot \alpha$ \State Let Outcome be the output of the tolerant tester of \cref{lem:tolerant-mean-tester} using sample set $\cS_j$ with parameters $\eps_1 = l_i$, $\eps_2 = 2l_i$, and $\delta = \delta'$ \If {Outcome is $\Accept$} \State Set $o_j = l_i$ and break \Comment{Escape inner loop for block $j$} \EndIf \EndFor \EndFor \If{there exists a $\Fail$ amongst $\{o_1, \ldots, o_w\}$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \ElsIf{$4 \sum_{j=1}^w o_j^2 \leq \alpha^2$} \State \Return $\OK$ \Comment{Note: $o_j$ is an estimate for $\| \bm{\mu}_{\bB_j} \|_2$} \Else \State \Return $\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot o_j$ \Comment{$\lambda$ is an estimate for $\| \bm{\mu} \|_1$} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-002: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The TestAndOptimizeMean algorithm.} \label{alg:testandoptimizemean} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, parameter $\beta \in [0, \frac{1}{2}]$, and sample access to $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \Statex Output: $\wh{\bm{\mu}} \in \R^d$ \State Define $k = \lceil d^{2 \beta} \rceil$, $\alpha = \eps \cdot \sqrt{\frac{k}{d}}$, $\zeta = 4 \eps \cdot \sqrt{d}$, and $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$ \Comment{Note: $\zeta > 2 \alpha$} \State Draw $m(k, \alpha, \delta')$ i.i.d.\ samples from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ and store it into a set $\cS$ \Comment{See \cref{def:m-d-alpha-delta}} \State Let Outcome be the output of the ApproxL1 algorithm given $\eps$, $\delta$, $k$, $\alpha$, $\zeta$, and $\bS$ as inputs \If{Outcome is $\OK$} \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\mu}} = \bm{0}$ \ElsIf{Outcome is $\lambda \in \R$ and $\lambda < \eps \sqrt{d}$} \State Draw $n \in \wt{\cO}(\lambda^2/\eps^4)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_n \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\mu}} = \argmin_{\| \bm{\beta} \|_1 \leq \lambda} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \| \by_i - \bm{\beta} \|_2^2$ \Comment{See \cref{eq:mean-estimation-program}} \Else \State Draw $n \in \wt{\cO}(d/\eps^2)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_n \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\mu}} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \by_i$ \Comment{Empirical mean} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-003: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The VectorizedApproxL1 algorithm.} \label{alg:vectorizedapproxl1} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, block size $k \in [d]$, lower bound $\alpha > 0$, upper bound $\zeta > 2 \alpha$, and i.i.d.\ samples $\cS$ from $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$, $\OK$, or $\lambda \in \R$ \State Define $w = \frac{10 d(d-1) \log d}{k(k-1)}$, $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$, and let $\bB_1, \ldots, \bB_w \subseteq [d]^2$ be a $(q=2, d, k, a=1, b = \cO(\frac{d \log d}{k}))$-partitioning scheme as per \cref{lem:probabilistic-partitioning-construction} \For{$j \in \{1, \ldots, w\}$} \State Define $\bS_{\bB_j} = \{ \bm{x}_{\bB_j} \in \R^{|\bB_j|} : \bm{x} \in \bS \}$ as the projected samples \Comment{See \cref{def:projected-vector}} \State Initialize $o_j = \Fail$ \For{$i = 1, 2, \ldots, \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil$} \State Define $l_i = 2^{i-1} \cdot \alpha$ \State Let Outcome be the output of the tolerant tester of \cref{lem:tolerant-covariance-tester} using sample set $\cS_{\bB_j}$ with $\eps_1 = l_i$, $\eps_2 = 2l_i$, and $\delta = \delta'$ \If {Outcome is $\Accept$} \State Set $o_j = l_i$ and break \Comment{Escape inner loop for block $j$} \EndIf \EndFor \EndFor \If{there exists a $\Fail$ amongst $\{o_1, \ldots, o_w\}$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \ElsIf{$4b \sum_{j=1}^w o_j^2 \leq \alpha^2$} \State \Return $\OK$ \Else \State \Return $\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot o_j$ \Comment{$\lambda$ is an estimate for $\| vec(\Sigma - \bB_d) \|_1$} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-004: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The TestAndOptimizeCovariance algorithm.} \label{alg:testandoptimizecovariance} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, parameter $\beta \in [0, \frac{1}{2}]$, and sample access to $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ \Statex Output: $\wh{\bm{\Sigma}} \in \R^{d \times d}$ \State Define $k = \lceil d^{2 \beta} \rceil$, $\alpha = \frac{\eps k}{d}$, $\zeta = 4 \eps d$, and $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$ \Comment{Note: $\zeta > 2 \alpha$} \State Draw $m'(k, \alpha, \delta')$ i.i.d.\ samples from $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ and store it into a set $\cS$ \Comment{See \cref{def:m-prime-d-alpha-delta}} \State Let Outcome be the output of the VectorizedApproxL1 algorithm given $\eps$, $\delta$, $k$, $\alpha$, $\zeta$, and $\bS$ as inputs \If{Outcome is $\OK$} \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\mu}} = \bI_d$ \ElsIf{Outcome is $\lambda \in \R$ and $\lambda < \eps d$} \State Draw $n \in \wt{\cO}(\lambda^2/\eps^4)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_n \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{0}, \bI_d)$ \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\Sigma}} = \argmin_{\substack{\text{$\bA \in \R^{d \times d}$ is p.s.d.} \| \vec(\bA - \bI_d) \|_1 \leq \lambda \lambda_{\min}(\bA) \geq 1}} \sum_{i=1}^n \| \bA - \by_i \by_i^\top \|_F^2$ \Comment{See \cref{eq:cov-estimation-program}} \Else \State Draw $2n \in \wt{\cO}(d^2/\eps^2)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_{2n} \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{0}, \bI_d)$ \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\Sigma}} = \frac{1}{2n} \sum_{i=1}^{2n} (\by_{2i} - \by_{2i-1}) (\by_{2i} - \by_{2i-1})^\top$ \Comment{Empirical covariance} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.sent-2411-283: In this section, we present an algorithm for testing whether an unknown distribution is close to a standard normal distribution.
elements.sent-2411-284: More specifically, we first describe a tolerant tester for the property that the mean of an isotropic Gaussian distribution equals zero.
elements.sent-2411-285: Subsequently, we present a tolerant tester for the property that the covariance matrix equals the identity matrix.
elements.sent-2411-286: The definition of a tolerant tester for the mean of an isotropic Gaussian distribution is given below.
elements.sent-2411-287: Suppose $\bm{\mu \in \R^d$ is a hidden mean vector and we draw $m$ samples $\bx_1, \ldots, \bx_m \sim N(\bm{\mu , \bI_d)$.}}
elements.sent-2411-288: An algorithm $ALG$ is said to be a $(\eps_1, \eps_2, \delta)$-tolerant isotropic Gaussian mean tester if it satisfies the following two conditions:.
elements.sent-2411-289: If $\| \bm{\mu \|_2 \leq \eps_1$, then $ALG$ should Accept with probability at least $1 - \delta$.}
elements.sent-2411-290: If $\| \bm{\mu \|_2 \geq \eps_2$, then $ALG$ should Reject with probability at least $1 - \delta$.}
elements.sent-2411-291: It is known that the test statistic $y_n = \left\|\frac{1 {\sqrt{n \sum_{i=1 ^{n \bx_i \right\|_2^2$ can be used for non-tolerant isotropic Gaussian mean testing with an appropriate threshold; see \cite[Appendix C]{DiakonikolasKaneStewart2017 .}}}}}}
elements.sent-2411-292: With the following lemma we show that $y_n$ can also be used for tolerant isotropic Gaussian mean testing.
elements.algorithm-2411-005: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The TolerantIGMT algorithm.} \label{alg:isotropic-gaussian-mean-testing} \Statex Input: $\eps_2 > \eps_1 > 0$, $\delta \in (0, 1)$, $m$ i.i.d.\ samples of $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$, where $\bm{\mu} \in \R^d$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$ (too little samples), $\Accept$ ($\| \bm{\mu} \|_2 \leq \eps_1$), or $\Reject$ ($\| \bm{\mu} \|_2 \geq \eps_2$). \State Define testing threshold $\tau = d + \frac{n(\eps_1^2 + \eps_2^2)}{2}$ \State Define sample batch size $n = \frac{16\sqrt{d}}{\eps_2^2 - \eps_1^2}$ \State Define number of rounds $r = \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ if $\left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ is odd, otherwise define $r = 1 + \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ \If{$m < nr$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \Else \For{$i \in \{1, \ldots, r\}$} \State Use an unused batch of $n$ i.i.d.\ samples $\bx^{(i)}_1, \ldots, \bx^{(i)}_n \sim N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \State Compute test statistic $y^{(i)}_n = \left\| \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \bx^{(i)}_i \right\|_2^2$ for the $i^{th}$ test \State Define $i^{th}$ outcome $R^{(i)}$ as $\Accept$ if $y^{(i)}_n \leq \tau$, and $\Reject$ otherwise \EndFor \State \Return $\maj(R^{(1)}, \ldots, R^{(r)})$ \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-006: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{TolerantZMGCT.} \label{alg:zero-mean-covariance-testing} \Statex Input: $\eps_2 > \eps_1 > 0$, $\delta \in (0,1)$, $m$ i.i.d.\ samples of $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$, where $\bm{\Sigma} \in \R^{d \times d}$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$ (too little samples), $\Accept$ ($\| \bm{\Sigma} - \bI_d \|_F^2 \leq \eps_1^2$), or $\Reject$ ($\| \bm{\Sigma} - \bI_d \|_F^2 \geq \eps_2^2$) \State Define testing threshold $\tau = \frac{\eps_2^2 + \eps_1^2}{2}$ \State Define sample batch size $n = 3200 \cdot d \cdot \max \left\{ \frac{1}{\eps_1^2}, \left( \frac{\eps_1^2}{\eps_2^2 - \eps_1^2} \right)^2, 2 \left( \frac{\eps_2}{\eps_2^2 - \eps_1^2} \right)^2\right\}$ \State Define number of rounds $r = \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ if $\left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ is odd, otherwise define $r = 1 + \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ \If{$m < nr$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \Else \For{$i \in \{1, \ldots, r\}$} \State Use an unused batch of $n$ i.i.d.\ samples $\bx^{(i)}_1, \ldots, \bx^{(i)}_n \sim N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ \State Compute test statistic $T^{(i)}_n$ according to \cref{def:test-statistic-Tn} for the $i^{th}$ test \State Define $i^{th}$ outcome $R^{(i)}$ as $\Accept$ if $T^{(i)}_n \leq \tau$, and $\Reject$ otherwise \EndFor \State \Return $\maj(R^{(1)}, \ldots, R^{(r)})$ \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-007: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The ApproxL1 algorithm.} \label{alg:approxl1} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, block size $k \in [d]$, lower bound $\alpha > 0$, upper bound $\zeta > 2 \alpha$, and i.i.d.\ samples $\cS$ from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$, $\OK$, or $\lambda \in \R$ \State Define $w = \left\lceil {d}/{k} \right\rceil$ and $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$ \State Partition the index set $[d]$ into $w$ blocks: \[ \bB_1 = \{1, \ldots, k\}, \bB_2 = \{k+1, \ldots, 2k\}, \ldots, \bB_w = \{k(w-1)+1, \ldots, d\} \] \For{$j \in \{1, \ldots, w\}$} \State Define $\cS_{j} = \{ \bx_{\bB_j} \in \R^{|\bB_j|} : \bx \in \cS \}$ as the samples projected to $\bB_j$ \Comment{See \cref{def:projected-vector}} \State Initialize $o_j = \Fail$ \For{$i = 1, 2, \ldots, \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil$} \State Define $l_i = 2^{i-1} \cdot \alpha$ \State Let Outcome be the output of the tolerant tester of \cref{lem:tolerant-mean-tester} using sample \Statex set $\cS_j$ with parameters $\eps_1 = l_i$, $\eps_2 = 2l_i$, and $\delta = \delta'$ \If {Outcome is $\Accept$} \State Set $o_j = l_i$ and break \Comment{Escape inner loop for block $j$} \EndIf \EndFor \EndFor \If{there exists a $\Fail$ amongst $\{o_1, \ldots, o_w\}$} \State\Return $\Fail$ \ElsIf{$4 \sum_{j=1}^w o_j^2 \leq \alpha^2$} \State\Return $\OK$ \Comment{Note: $o_j$ is an estimate for $\| \bm{\mu}_{\bB_j} \|_2$} \Else \Return $\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot o_j$ \Comment{$\lambda$ is an estimate for $\| \bm{\mu} \|_1$} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-008: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The TestAndOptimizeMean algorithm.} \label{alg:testandoptimizemean} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, parameter $\eta \in [0, \frac{1}{4}]$, and sample access to $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \Statex Output: $\wh{\bm{\mu}} \in \R^d$ \State Define $k = \lceil d^{4\eta}\rceil$, $\alpha = \eps\cdot d^{-(1-3\eta)/2}$, $\zeta = 4 \eps \cdot \sqrt{d}$, and $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{\lceil d/k \rceil \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$ \Comment{Note: $\zeta > 2 \alpha$} \State Draw $m(k, \alpha, \delta')$ i.i.d.\ samples from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ and store it into a set $\cS$ \Comment{See \cref{def:m-d-alpha-delta}} \State Let Outcome be the output of the ApproxL1 algorithm given $k$, $\alpha$, $\zeta$, and $\bS$ as inputs \If{Outcome is $\lambda \in \R$ and $\lambda < \eps \sqrt{d}$} \State Draw $n \in \wt{\cO}(\lambda^2/\eps^4)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_n \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \State return $\wh{\bm{\mu}} = \argmin_{\| \bm{\beta} \|_1 \leq \lambda} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \| \by_i - \bm{\beta} \|_2^2$ \Comment{See \cref{eq:mean-estimation-program}} \Else \State Draw $n \in \wt{\cO}(d/\eps^2)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_n \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \State return $\wh{\bm{\mu}} = \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \by_i$ \Comment{Empirical mean} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-009: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The VectorizedApproxL1 algorithm.} \label{alg:vectorizedapproxl1} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, block size $k \in [d]$, lower bound $\alpha > 0$, upper bound $\zeta > 2 \alpha$, and i.i.d.\ samples $\cS$ from $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$ or $\lambda \in \R$ \State Define $w = \frac{10 d(d-1) \log d}{k(k-1)}$, $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$, and let $\bB_1, \ldots, \bB_w \subseteq [d]^2$ be a $(q=2, d, k, a=1, b = \cO(\frac{d \log d}{k}))$-partitioning scheme as per \cref{lem:probabilistic-partitioning-construction} \For{$j \in \{1, \ldots, w\}$} \State Define $\bS_{\bB_j} = \{ \bm{x}_{\bB_j} \in \R^{|\bB_j|} : \bm{x} \in \bS \}$ as the projected samples \Comment{See \cref{def:projected-vector}} \State Initialize $o_j = \Fail$ \For{$i = 1, 2, \ldots, \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil$} \State Define $l_i = 2^{i-1} \cdot \alpha$ \State Let Outcome be the output of the tolerant tester of \cref{lem:tolerant-covariance-tester} using sample set $\cS_{\bB_j}$ with $\eps_1 = l_i$, \Statex $\eps_2 = 2l_i$, and $\delta = \delta'$ \If {Outcome is $\Accept$} \State Set $o_j = l_i$ and break \Comment{Escape inner loop for block $j$} \EndIf \EndFor \EndFor \If{there exists a $\Fail$ amongst $\{o_1, \ldots, o_w\}$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \Else \State \Return $\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot o_j$ \Comment{$\lambda$ is an estimate for $\| vec(\Sigma - \bB_d) \|_1$} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-010: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The TestAndOptimizeCovariance algorithm.} \label{alg:testandoptimizecovariance} \Statex Input: Error rate $\eps > 0$, failure rate $\delta \in (0,1)$, parameter $\eta \in [0, 1]$, and sample access to $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ \Statex Output: $\wh{\bm{\Sigma}} \in \R^{d \times d}$ \State Define $k = \lceil d^{\eta} \rceil$, $\alpha = \eps d^{-(2 - \eta)/2}$, $\zeta = 4 \eps d$, and $\delta' = \frac{\delta}{w \cdot \lceil \log_2 \zeta/\alpha \rceil}$ \Comment{Note: $\zeta > 2 \alpha$} \State Draw $m'(k, \alpha, \delta')$ i.i.d.\ samples from $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ and store it into a set $\cS$ \Comment{See \cref{def:m-prime-d-alpha-delta}} \State Let Outcome be the output of the VectorizedApproxL1 algorithm given $\eps$, $\delta$, $k$, $\alpha$, $\zeta$, and $\bS$ as inputs \If{Outcome is $\lambda \in \R$ and $\lambda < \eps d$} \State Draw $n \in \wt{\cO}(\lambda^2/\eps^4)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_n \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{0}, \bI_d)$ \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\Sigma}} = \argmin_{\substack{\text{$\bA \in \R^{d \times d}$ is p.s.d.} \| \vec(\bA - \bI_d) \|_1 \leq \lambda \lambda_{\min}(\bA) \geq 1}} \sum_{i=1}^n \| \bA - \by_i \by_i^\top \|_F^2$ \Comment{See \cref{eq:cov-estimation-program}} \Else \State Draw $2n \in \wt{\cO}(d^2/\eps^2)$ i.i.d.\ samples $\by_1, \ldots, \by_{2n} \in \R^d$ from $N(\bm{0}, \bI_d)$ \State \Return $\wh{\bm{\Sigma}} = \frac{1}{2n} \sum_{i=1}^{2n} (\by_{2i} - \by_{2i-1}) (\by_{2i} - \by_{2i-1})^\top$ \Comment{Empirical covariance} \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.sent-2411-571: Our simple script is given in \cref{sec:appendix-python-code .}
elements.sent-2411-572: We perform two experiments on multivariate Gaussians of dimension $d = 500$ while varying two parameters: sparsity $s \in [d]$ and advice quality $q \in \R_{\geq 0}$.
elements.sent-2411-573: In both experiments, the difference vector $\bm{\mu - \wt{\bm{\mu \in \R^d$ is generated with random $\pm q/s$ values in the first $s$ coordinates and zeros in the remaining $d-s$ coordinates.}}}
elements.sent-2411-574: In the first experiment (see \cref{fig:exp1 ), we fix $q = 50$ and vary $s \in \{100, 200, 300\}$.}
elements.sent-2411-575: In the second experiment (see \cref{fig:exp2 ), we fix $s = 100$ and vary $q \in \{0.1, 20, 30\}$.}
elements.sent-2411-576: In both experiments, we see that TestAndOptimize beats the empirical mean estimate in terms of incurred $\ell_2$ error (which translate directly to $\tv$), with the diminishing benefits as $q$ or $s$ increases.
elements.sent-2411-577: While running our experiments, we observed an interesting phenomenon: the rate of improvement does not worsen as $\ell_1$ increases if we fixed the $\ell_0$ sparsity; see \cref{fig:exp3 .}
elements.sent-2411-578: As such, it would be interesting to show theoretical guarantees with advice error in the $\ell_0$-norm.
elements.sent-2411-579: For computational efficiency, we solve the LASSO optimization in its Lagrangian form \begin{equation}\wh{\mu} = \argmin_{\beta \in \R^d} \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^n \| \by_i - \beta \|_2^2 + \lambda \|\beta\|_1\end{equation} using the LassoLarsCV method in scikit-learn, instead of the equivalent penalized form.
elements.sent-2411-580: The value of the hyperparameter $\lambda$ is chosen using 5-fold cross-validation.

elements.algorithm-2411-011: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{The TolerantIGMT algorithm.} \label{alg:isotropic-gaussian-mean-testing} \Statex Input: $\eps_2 > \eps_1 > 0$, $\delta \in (0, 1)$, $m$ i.i.d.\ samples of $N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$, where $\bm{\mu} \in \R^d$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$ (too little samples), $\Accept$ ($\| \bm{\mu} \|_2 \leq \eps_1$), or $\Reject$ ($\| \bm{\mu} \|_2 \geq \eps_2$). \State Define sample batch size $n = \lceil \frac{16\sqrt{d}}{\eps_2^2 - \eps_1^2} \rceil$ \State Define number of rounds $r = \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ if $\left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ is odd, otherwise define $r = 1 + \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ \State Define testing threshold $\tau = d + \frac{n(\eps_1^2 + \eps_2^2)}{2}$ \If{$m < nr$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \Else \For{$i \in \{1, \ldots, r\}$} \State Use an unused batch of $n$ i.i.d.\ samples $\bx^{(i)}_1, \ldots, \bx^{(i)}_n \sim N(\bm{\mu}, \bI_d)$ \State Compute test statistic $y^{(i)}_n = \left\| \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \bx^{(i)}_i \right\|_2^2$ for the $i^{th}$ test \State Define $i^{th}$ outcome $R^{(i)}$ as $\Accept$ if $y^{(i)}_n \leq \tau$, and $\Reject$ otherwise \EndFor \State \Return $\maj(R^{(1)}, \ldots, R^{(r)})$ \EndIf \end{algorithmic}

elements.algorithm-2411-012: \begin{algorithmic}[1] \caption{TolerantZMGCT.} \label{alg:zero-mean-covariance-testing} \Statex Input: $\eps_2 > \eps_1 > 0$, $\delta \in (0,1)$, $m$ i.i.d.\ samples of $N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$, where $\bm{\Sigma} \in \R^{d \times d}$ \Statex Output: $\Fail$ (too little samples), $\Accept$ ($\| \bm{\Sigma} - \bI_d \|_F^2 \leq \eps_1^2$), or $\Reject$ ($\| \bm{\Sigma} - \bI_d \|_F^2 \geq \eps_2^2$) \State Define sample batch size $n = \left\lceil 3200 \cdot d \cdot \max \left\{ \frac{1}{\eps_1^2}, \left( \frac{\eps_1^2}{\eps_2^2 - \eps_1^2} \right)^2, 2 \left( \frac{\eps_2}{\eps_2^2 - \eps_1^2} \right)^2\right\} \right\rceil$ \State Define number of rounds $r = \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ if $\left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ is odd, otherwise define $r = 1 + \left\lceil \log(\frac{12}{\delta}) \right\rceil$ \State Define testing threshold $\tau = \frac{\eps_2^2 + \eps_1^2}{2}$ \If{$m < nr$} \State \Return $\Fail$ \Else \For{$i \in \{1, \ldots, r\}$} \State Use an unused batch of $n$ i.i.d.\ samples $\bx^{(i)}_1, \ldots, \bx^{(i)}_n \sim N(\bm{0}, \bm{\Sigma})$ \State Compute test statistic $T^{(i)}_n$ according to \cref{def:test-statistic-Tn} for the $i^{th}$ test \State Define $i^{th}$ outcome $R^{(i)}$ as $\Accept$ if $T^{(i)}_n \leq \tau$, and $\Reject$ otherwise \EndFor \State \Return $\maj(R^{(1)}, \ldots, R^{(r)})$ \EndIf \end{algorithmic}
elements.sent-2411-582: When ApproxL1 outputs $\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1 ^w \sqrt{|\bB_j| \cdot o_j \in \R$, we can lower bound $\lambda$ as follows: \begin{equation}\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot o_j \geq 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot \frac{\| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_2}{2} \tag{since $\| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_2 \leq 2 o_j$} \geq \sum_{j=1}^w \| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_1 \tag{since $\| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_1 \leq \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot \| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_2$} = \| \mu \|_1 \tag{since $\sum_{j=1}^w \| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_1 = \| \mu_{\bB_j} \|_1$}\end{equation} That is, $\lambda \geq \| \bm{\mu \|_1$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-583: When VectorizedApproxL1 outputs $\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1 ^w \sqrt{|\bB_j| \cdot o_j \in \R$, we can lower bound $\lambda$ as follows: \begin{equation}\lambda = 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot o_j \geq 2 \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j|} \cdot \frac{\| \Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d \|_F}{2} \tag{since $\| \Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d \|_F \leq 2 o_j$} = \sum_{j=1}^w \sqrt{|\bB_j| \cdot \| \vec(\Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d) \|_2^2} \tag{since $\| \Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d \|_F^2 = \| \vec(\Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d) \|_2^2$} \geq \sum_{j=1}^w \| \vec(\Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d) \|_1 \tag{since $\| \vec(\Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d) \|_1^2 \leq |\bB_j| \cdot \| \vec(\Sigma_{\bB_j} - \bI_d) \|_2^2$} \geq \| \vec(\Sigma - \bI_d) \|_1 \tag{Since each cell in $\Sigma$ appears at least $a=1$ times across all submatrices $\Sigma_{\bB_1}, \ldots, \Sigma_{\bB_w}$}\end{equation} That is, $\lambda \geq \| \vec(\bm{\Sigma - \bI_d) \|_1$.}}}

elements.lstlisting-2411-001: [language=Python, caption={Python script for experiments}, label={lst:python_script}] import numpy as np import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import pickle import sys from sklearn import linear_model from tqdm import tqdm from typing import Tuple def estimate(samples: np.ndarray) -> Tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray]: N, d = samples.shape X = np.concatenate([np.identity(d) for _ in range(N)]) y = np.concatenate(samples) reg = linear_model.LassoLarsCV(cv=5) reg.fit(X, y) opt_est = reg.coef_ emp_est = 1./N * sum(y_i for y_i in samples) return opt_est, emp_est def run_experiments( rng: np.random.Generator, d: int, s: int, q: float, Nmin: int, Nmax: int, Nstep: int, Nrepeats: int, fname: str ) -> None: assert 0 <= s and s <= d # Generate random ground truth mu mu = [0 for _ in range(d)] for i in range(s): mu[i] = q/s * rng.choice([-1, 1]) mu = np.array(mu) # Run N_vals = np.arange(Nmin, Nmax+1, Nstep) opt_err = [[] for _ in range(Nrepeats)] emp_err = [[] for _ in range(Nrepeats)] for run_idx in tqdm(range(Nrepeats)): samples = rng.multivariate_normal(mu, np.identity(d), size=Nmax) for N in tqdm(N_vals): opt_est, emp_est = estimate(samples[:N]) opt_err[run_idx].append(np.linalg.norm(opt_est - mu, 2)) emp_err[run_idx].append(np.linalg.norm(emp_est - mu, 2)) # Save results results = [N_vals, opt_err, emp_err] with open("{0}.pkl".format(fname), 'wb') as file: pickle.dump(results, file) # Generate plot generate_plot(fname) def generate_plot(fname: str) -> None: with open("{0}.pkl".format(fname), 'rb') as file: results = pickle.load(file) N_vals, opt_err, emp_err = results opt_mean = np.mean(opt_err, axis=0) opt_std = np.std(opt_err, axis=0) emp_mean = np.mean(emp_err, axis=0) emp_std = np.std(emp_err, axis=0) plt.plot(N_vals, opt_mean, label="TestAndOptimize", color='g') plt.plot(N_vals, emp_mean, label="Empirical", color='r') plt.fill_between(N_vals, opt_mean - opt_std, opt_mean + opt_std, color='g', alpha=0.5) plt.fill_between(N_vals, emp_mean - emp_std, emp_mean + emp_std, color='r', alpha=0.5) plt.xlabel("Number of samples") plt.ylabel(r"$\ell_2$ error") plt.legend() plt.savefig("{0}.png".format(fname), dpi=300, bbox_inches='tight') if __name__ == "__main__": mode = int(sys.argv[1]) d = int(sys.argv[2]) s = int(sys.argv[3]) q = float(sys.argv[4]) seed = 314159 rng = np.random.default_rng(seed) Nmin = 10 Nmax = 300 Nstep = 10 Nrepeats = 10 fname = "plot_d{0}_sparsity{1}_L1norm{2}_Nmax={3}_runs={4}".format(d, s, q, Nmax, Nrepeats) if mode == 0: run_experiments(rng, d, s, q, Nmin, Nmax, Nstep, Nrepeats, fname) elif mode == 1: generate_plot(fname) else: raise ValueError("Invalid mode. Use '0' for full run and '1' for just plotting.")
